differences.title: 1. EASE OF USE
differences.leftDesc: Easier for prototyping and research, feels more natural to Python developers.
differences.rightDesc: Steeper learning curve initially but offers clear advantages for deployment and optimization.

differences.title: 2. DEVELOPMENT SPEED
differences.leftDesc: Dynamic graphs allow for quicker experimentation and debugging during the development phase.
differences.rightDesc: Debugging static graphs can be more challenging, requiring specific tools and approaches.

differences.title: 3. COMMUNITY & SUPPORT
differences.leftDesc: Strong community in academic research, many cutting-edge papers use PyTorch.
differences.rightDesc: Vast community support, extensive documentation, and widespread industry adoption.

differences.title: 4. DEPLOYMENT
differences.leftDesc: Deployment options exist but are less mature and integrated than TensorFlow's.
differences.rightDesc: Robust tools like TensorFlow Serving and Lite for seamless production deployment.

differences.title: 5. MODEL BUILDING
differences.leftDesc: Define-by-run approach, code executes as written, making it intuitive.
differences.rightDesc: Define-and-run approach, graph is built first, then executed for optimization.

differences.title: 6. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Rapidly expanding libraries and tools, but still catching up to TensorFlow.
differences.rightDesc: Comprehensive suite of tools: TensorBoard, TensorFlow Lite, TensorFlow.js, etc.

differences.title: 7. HARDWARE ACCELERATION
differences.leftDesc: Leverages GPUs effectively for training and inference, excellent CUDA integration.
differences.rightDesc: Optimized for Google's TPUs, offering unparalleled performance on specialized hardware.
